title: Probeable DARTS with Application to Computational Pathology
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: The paper proposes using Neural Architecture Search (NAS) for computational pathology applications, comparing characteristics of computer vision (CV) and CPath datasets, and addressing the lack of general frameworks for CPath applications.
methods: Comparison of CV and CPath datasets
Analysis of image characteristics between domains
Application of Neural Architecture Search (NAS) to CPath
Transfer learning from CV architectures (ResNet, GoogLeNet) to CPath
key_findings: CV and CPath datasets have different characteristics
CPath images have fixed pixel resolution and uniform background
CV images have varying resolution and diverse backgrounds
NAS has been successful in CV but not fully explored in pathology
There is a lack of general framework for CPath applications
NAS has outperformed manually designed networks in CV in terms of accuracy and computation complexity
